description:
You are an automation-workflow specialist.  
Inside the current Apptainer workspace you already have a directory

    /home/user/logs/

and, in that directory, a text file named

    /home/user/logs/webserver_access.log

The file follows the classic Apache “combined” access-log format (one entry per line).  
Your objective is to produce a *filtered* log that contains **only** those requests whose
returned HTTP status code is in the 5 × × range (500–599).

Required actions and acceptance criteria:

1. Using **exactly one** terminal command (i.e. a single pipeline that you press <Enter> once for),
   read /home/user/logs/webserver_access.log and write the matching 5 × × lines—preserving their
   original order—into a new file called

       /home/user/logs/errors_only.log

2. The regular-expression filter must match any status code whose first digit is “5”.
   (A correct regex would be something like 5[0-9]{2}, but the precise pattern
   is up to you as long as it is accurate.)

3. Formatting rules for /home/user/logs/errors_only.log that the automated tests will check:
   • It must contain *only* the matching log lines—no header, footer, blank lines or extra whitespace.  
   • Lines must appear in exactly the same order as in the original file.  
   • Each line must end with a single UNIX newline character (LF).  
   • The file must be world-readable (default permissions from redirection are fine; no root privileges are needed).  

4. Do **not** modify or delete /home/user/logs/webserver_access.log.

5. Once the command finishes, there is no further manual editing—/home/user/logs/errors_only.log
   should already be correct.

Your entire task is therefore to craft and execute one appropriate command that satisfies the criteria above.
The automated grader will look exclusively at the contents of /home/user/logs/errors_only.log to verify success.
truth:
Pre-existing artefacts before the agent starts:

1. Directory:
   /home/user/logs            (normal user ownership, default 0755)

2. File: /home/user/logs/webserver_access.log
   Exact contents (10 lines, each terminated with LF):

127.0.0.1 - - [10/Oct/2022:13:55:36 +0000] "GET /index.html HTTP/1.1" 200 1024 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
192.168.1.10 - - [10/Oct/2022:14:00:01 +0000] "POST /login HTTP/1.1" 302 512 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
10.0.0.5 - - [10/Oct/2022:14:02:16 +0000] "GET /doesnotexist HTTP/1.1" 404 256 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
127.0.0.1 - - [10/Oct/2022:14:05:45 +0000] "GET /api/data HTTP/1.1" 500 128 "-" "curl/7.68.0"
203.0.113.55 - - [10/Oct/2022:14:06:12 +0000] "PUT /api/update HTTP/1.1" 503 64 "-" "Python-requests/2.25.1"
192.0.2.44 - - [10/Oct/2022:14:07:30 +0000] "DELETE /api/delete HTTP/1.1" 204 0 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
198.51.100.23 - - [10/Oct/2022:14:08:59 +0000] "GET /reports HTTP/1.1" 504 32 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
203.0.113.77 - - [10/Oct/2022:14:10:42 +0000] "GET /admin HTTP/1.1" 403 128 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
192.168.1.15 - - [10/Oct/2022:14:12:03 +0000] "POST /submit HTTP/1.1" 201 256 "-" "curl/7.68.0"
10.1.1.1 - - [10/Oct/2022:14:13:27 +0000] "GET /status HTTP/1.1" 502 16 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"

3. No file named /home/user/logs/errors_only.log exists at start.

Expected artefacts after successful task completion:

1. /home/user/logs/errors_only.log
   Contents must be *exactly* the following four lines, in the given order, each terminated with LF:

127.0.0.1 - - [10/Oct/2022:14:05:45 +0000] "GET /api/data HTTP/1.1" 500 128 "-" "curl/7.68.0"
203.0.113.55 - - [10/Oct/2022:14:06:12 +0000] "PUT /api/update HTTP/1.1" 503 64 "-" "Python-requests/2.25.1"
198.51.100.23 - - [10/Oct/2022:14:08:59 +0000] "GET /reports HTTP/1.1" 504 32 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
10.1.1.1 - - [10/Oct/2022:14:13:27 +0000] "GET /status HTTP/1.1" 502 16 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"

No other files need to be created or altered.
